channel.title: Bayram Onder Kan — Blog
channel.description: Pittsburgh real estate insights, neighborhood guides, and market updates.

item.title: Pittsburgh Real Estate Market Update — June 2026
item.description: Mid-2026 check-in on Pittsburgh: steady prices near $260K, a more balanced pace, and 30-year rates easing to 6.47% — with the data and sources behind it.

item.title: Penn Hills, PA: Space, Variety and Mid-Range Prices 10 Miles East of the City
item.description: One of the region's biggest suburbs, Penn Hills offers a wide range of homes at moderate prices with quick highway access. Here's how to target the right pocket.

item.title: Cranberry Township, PA: Top Schools, Big Amenities and Steady Demand
item.description: One of the region's most sought-after suburbs, Cranberry pairs Seneca Valley schools with deep amenities — at the region's higher price points. Here's the full look.

item.title: New Homes Are Now $30,000 Cheaper Than Existing Homes — What Pittsburgh Buyers Should Know
item.description: For the first time in recent memory, new construction is selling below existing homes. Here's why the gap flipped — and what it means for Pittsburgh.

item.title: Avalon, PA: A Walkable Ohio River Borough in the North Boroughs
item.description: Tidy streets, classic homes and Northgate schools just 6 miles from Downtown — here's why Avalon stays popular with first-time buyers and small investors.

item.title: Mt. Oliver, PA: A Tiny, Affordable Borough Surrounded by the City
item.description: Completely ringed by Pittsburgh just south of the South Side, Mt. Oliver pairs very low prices with a city-adjacent location. Here's the buyer-and-investor picture.

item.title: Mortgage Rates Hold Steady at 6.36% — What Pittsburgh Buyers Should Know Right Now
item.description: Rates haven't moved much lately. Here's how to think about a steady 6.36% if you're buying or selling in Pittsburgh right now.

item.title: Sheraden, Pittsburgh: An Affordable West End Streetcar Suburb 6 Miles From Downtown
item.description: Low prices and a quick city commute have drawn new residents to Sheraden. Here's what buyers and investors should know about this hilly West End neighborhood.

item.title: HUD Clarifies: Agents Can Discuss Crime Statistics and Schools — Here's What That Means for Pittsburgh Buyers
item.description: HUD confirmed agents can discuss crime data and school information — without discriminatory intent. Here's what that means for your search.

item.title: Uptown, Pittsburgh: The EcoInnovation District and a Neighborhood to Watch
item.description: Wedged between Downtown and Oakland, Uptown has drawn $880M+ in investment. Here's why it's promising — and why it demands careful research.

item.title: 64% of Pennsylvania Homeowners Find Homeownership Stressful — Here's the Honest Conversation We Should Have
item.description: The stress is real, but it's manageable when you go in with the right expectations. Here's the honest conversation I'd rather have before you buy.

item.title: McKees Rocks, PA: Low Prices, River Heritage and Revitalization Near the City
item.description: A former industrial and railroad hub just 6 miles from Downtown, McKees Rocks offers some of the area's lowest prices — for buyers ready to do their homework.

item.title: Mortgage Applications Are Rising — What That Tells Us About Pittsburgh's 2026 Market
item.description: Everyone watches rates, but application volume tells a more honest story. Right now it's rising — and that matters for buyers and sellers alike.

item.title: Munhall, PA: Steel History, Affordable Homes and The Waterfront Next Door
item.description: In the heart of the historic Homestead steel district, Munhall pairs affordable homes with one of the region's biggest shopping destinations — here's the full picture.

item.title: Pittsburgh Real Estate: What the Numbers Are Telling Us This April 2026
item.description: Prices are up, days on market are up too — but only for homes priced on wishful thinking. Here's the honest April 2026 read, neighborhood by neighborhood.

item.title: 4 Features That Sell a Home Faster
item.description: PA Realtors identified the four features that consistently help homes sell quicker. Here's how to use them whether you're listing or buying in Pittsburgh.

item.title: Glassport, PA: A Quiet River Borough With Some of Allegheny County's Lowest Prices
item.description: Named for the glass industry that built it, Glassport is an affordable, tight-knit Mon River community — here's what buyers and investors should weigh.

item.title: McKeesport, PA: Affordable Riverfront Living Where Due Diligence Pays Off
item.description: Once a 55,000-person steel hub, McKeesport now offers some of the metro's lowest prices — a real opportunity for buyers and investors who do their homework.

item.title: Valencia, PA: Small-Town Calm in One of the North's Fastest-Growing Corridors
item.description: A tiny Butler County borough with Mars Area schools and quick access to the booming Route 228 / I-79 corridor — here's who Valencia really fits.

item.title: The Ultimate Guide to Planning Your Home Renovation
item.description: A clear, step-by-step framework for planning a renovation that fits your life and protects your home's value — from defining your vision to settling in.

item.title: How a Recent Fed Rate Cut Could Impact Pittsburgh's Mortgage Rates and Housing Affordability
item.description: When the Fed lowers rates, mortgage rates often follow. Here's how a rate cut could ripple through Pittsburgh's housing market — and what actually drives the outcome.

item.title: Fight Your Property Tax Bill: A Comprehensive Guide to Appealing Assessments in Allegheny County (PA)
item.description: Allegheny County homeowners can challenge their assessed value every year — with no filing fee. Here's how to build a strong appeal, step by step.

item.title: Pittsburgh's Emerald Gems: Exploring the City's Breathtaking Public Parks
item.description: From Frick to Schenley to a reclaimed coal mine turned green haven, Pittsburgh's parks are part of what makes a neighborhood feel like home. Here's a tour.

item.title: North Hills, Pittsburgh: Where Suburban Charm Meets City Convenience
item.description: Peaceful neighborhoods, top-rated schools, and a short commute to downtown. Here's what makes the North Hills suburbs such a sought-after place to live.

item.title: Embracing the Charm of Wexford, PA: My Home Since 2016
item.description: A personal note on why my family has loved calling Wexford home since 2016 — the community, the natural beauty, the schools, and the small-town charm.

item.title: Navigating the North Allegheny County Real Estate Market in 2024
item.description: North Allegheny County still favors sellers — low inventory, fast sales, top schools. Here's what buyers and sellers should expect, and the lifestyle behind the numbers.

item.title: Navigating the Steel City's Streets: A Look at Pittsburgh Real Estate in 2024
item.description: A tour of Pittsburgh's 2024 market — steady price growth, tight inventory, and the up-and-coming neighborhoods worth a closer look.

item.title: Bayram Önder Kan's Value Proposition
item.description: What you can expect when you work with me: local expertise, real listening, hard work, and a personalized approach built to make your real estate journey seamless.
